description_long: This object is a compact handheld two-way radio, commonly known as a walkie-talkie, featuring a prominent black antenna extending from the top. The front face displays a numeric keypad with light-colored buttons and a small display screen above it, surrounded by a dark plastic casing. The sides and back of the device are accented with yellow and brownish tape, possibly for grip or repair, and the rear has handwritten markings. The top section includes a rotary knob and a connector port, likely for volume control and accessory attachment. The device appears rugged, suitable for outdoor or urban use, and is designed for easy one-handed operation. Its overall construction suggests durability and portability, ideal for communication in urban environments.
description: A rugged handheld walkie-talkie with a black antenna, numeric keypad, and yellow tape accents, designed for portable communication.
description_view_0: Front view shows the numeric keypad, display screen, and the full length of the antenna.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thickness of the device, yellow tape, and the protruding antenna.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals handwritten markings, brown tape, and the back casing details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays additional yellow tape and the side profile of the device.
